title: ED altered mental status documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.
assessment.rationale: The exact NICE delirium indicators support documenting recent onset or fluctuation, associated cognitive, perceptual, physical-function or social-behaviour change, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context in adults at risk; they do not cover every altered-mental-status cause.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 18 years and over in hospital or long-term residential or nursing care.
source.setting: Hospital and long-term care; the acute-hospital recommendations are partially applicable to emergency assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact only for recent fluctuating cognitive, perceptual, physical-function and social-behaviour changes, documentation of those changes, and use of a competent structured assessment. It does not establish delirium or cover every cause of altered mental status.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

assessment.text: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports documenting observed changes and a competent structured assessment only when actually performed; it does not auto-select an examination or diagnosis.
